title: "I Will Not Destroy It For Ten's Sake" Genesis 18:16-33

notes.introduction: An exposition of Genesis 18:16-33 on Abraham's intercession before God over the pending judgment of Sodom and Gomorrah, set within the broader narrative of Lot's presence in Sodom and God's covenant relationship with Abraham. The passage is read alongside Old Testament and New Testament texts to trace how recipients of divine information have responded to knowledge of coming judgment.

sections.heading: Background: Lot, Sodom, and Prior Events
Information Provided: Why God Tells Abraham
The Sin and Pending Judgment of Sodom
Abraham's Intercession: From Fifty to Ten
Old Testament Responses to Divine Information: Elisha and Jonah
Paul's Response: Sorrow, Warning, and Active Proclamation
The Great White Throne and the Call to Respond
